overall_reasoning: The patch implements exactly the requested capability with a conservative security posture: fallback only on NoAttestations, acceptance only when every regular member verifies, and preservation of the pre-existing NoAttestations signal so the SLSA/absence path is not regressed. The lock-time condition change is necessary to let per-file attestations be discovered instead of permanently caching an archive-digest 'unavailable' result. Tests are genuinely thorough and policy-focused, the implementation reuses repository infrastructure, and the trajectory shows the finished change committed with focused tests, fmt, and clippy -D warnings passing; the broader mise task failure was solely due to unavailable network dependencies and is not attributable to the patch. No correctness or safety blockers identified.

rating_rationales.code_clarity: Structure is readable with a clear doc comment stating the fallback precondition. The hand-rolled AttestationVerifier trait with lifetime-bound method is somewhat awkward (needed only for test injection), and per-file verification is sequential, but naming and error messages are precise.
rating_rationales.repository_fit: Policy lives in the existing sole-bridge module (src/github/sigstore.rs), reuses file::untar/TarFormat/TarOptions and existing tempfile/walkdir/tar dependencies, and follows the repo's pattern of distinguishing NoAttestations from hard verification errors for SLSA fallback.
rating_rationales.scope_discipline: Changes are limited to the sigstore bridge, the github backend call sites, and a small justified artifact_format extraction shared with install_artifact. No unrelated edits; the trajectory shows only the three intended files committed.
rating_rationales.security_and_safety: Fails closed on every ambiguous outcome, rejects symlinks and special files in extracted trees, verifies into an auto-cleaned tempdir, and checksum verification still precedes provenance verification. Extraction of an unverified archive is inherent to the feature and uses the same sanitizer-equipped untar path as installation, so it introduces no new exposure; sequential per-file API queries could be slow or rate-limit-prone for large archives but are safe.
rating_rationales.test_quality: Eleven focused tests exercise primary success, no-fallback-on-rejection, full/partial/zero member coverage, API errors, invalid archives, non-archives, empty archives, and symlinks, using a mock verifier that records call order. Minor gaps: only the tar extraction path is exercised (zip/7z untar branches untested) and no end-to-end test against real attestation bundles, which is impractical offline.

strengths: Fallback triggers only on NoAttestations for a recognized archive format; archive-level verification remains primary and a rejected archive-level attestation never falls back

Fail-closed policy is explicit and consistent: partial member coverage, extraction errors, API errors, cryptographic failures, symlinks/special files, and invalid archives all reject; only complete per-file coverage accepts
Zero-attestation cases still return the original NoAttestations signal, preserving the existing SLSA/absence fallback and avoiding a behavior regression for ordinary archives
Both install-time and current-platform lock-time GitHub verification paths share the same policy via the central sigstore bridge, preventing divergence

The lockfile interaction was thought through: current-platform lock now runs full verification even when the pre-download probe reports the archive digest unattested, enabling per-file discovery instead of caching a false 'unavailable'

Eleven new focused tests with an injected verifier seam cover primary/short-circuit, success, and every failure mode, including a cfg(unix) symlink rejection test; format resolution is shared with install_artifact rather than duplicated
Changes are confined to three intended files, committed as one scoped commit with a clean worktree; focused tests, fmt --check, and clippy -D warnings pass per the trajectory
summary: Adds a fail-closed per-file attestation fallback for GitHub release archives in the central sigstore bridge: the archive blob is verified first, and only when it has no attestations does the code extract to a tempdir and require every regular member to verify. Existing SLSA/absence behavior is preserved when nothing is attested anywhere. Tests, formatting, and clippy pass; work is committed. Mergeable.
